audit_items.proof_trace: malloc() at line 43 is not checked for NULL before values[i] is first dereferenced at line 46; if malloc fails, this is an immediate null pointer dereference. However, mtest2.c is a standalone test utility (not part of the LDAP server), so external LDAP attackers cannot reach this code path, and the allocation size (~64–447 ints) is small enough that malloc failure is extremely unlikely in practice.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An operator running the mtest2 standalone binary in a severely memory-constrained environment would trigger a NULL dereference at line 46 (values[i] = rand()%1024) when malloc returns NULL, causing a crash; this is not reachable via LDAP.
